key_points: Freshman dorms are notoriously hot with no real cooling
West-facing tower rooms bake in afternoon sun daily
Dorms unchanged for 35+ years despite repeated complaints
key_quotes: Those dorms are HOT as hell! And if your room is on the west side of a tower, you will bake in the afternoon sun.
On hot and humid nights, those dorm rooms are miserable.
Sorry about the cons of going to UH Manoa... I hope the situations get better for you.

key_points: Campus is notably dead at night — very little happening after dark
Most local students work part-time or full-time, limiting social scene
Greek life is minimal — only two fraternities on campus
key_quotes: There are several reasons why there's not a whole lot going on at UH Manoa, especially at night — the vast majority of students must work, at least part-time.
UH only offers two fraternities: Kappa Sigma and Tau Kappa Epsilon.
I have to work a lot of hours in order to support myself, pay tuition — there's just not a lot of time for social activities.

key_points: Food trucks on campus are a genuine highlight worth knowing about
Dining hall has late-night options but nothing memorable
Meal swipes usability at Starbucks and Jamba Juice unclear to students
key_quotes: Not sure if they still have food trucks on campus, but those were awesome.
Gateway would have late night snacks/meals and was pretty much always busy.
For the meal swipes, can they be used at the Starbucks and Jamba Juice or does it only apply to the actual dining hall?

key_points: Cost of living stress is real — many students are paycheck to paycheck
Island isolation and 'island fever' is a genuine concern for mainlanders
No direct mentions of counseling services in student data
key_quotes: Do you experience island fever? All of my friends/family say living in Hawaii would get boring and isolating really quickly.
The middle class no longer exists. Young people no longer have a sense of autonomy.
I previously attended the University of Hawaii my freshman year. After doing badly there, I was suspended for a semester.

key_points: Out-of-state tuition is steep; WUE discount helps but still expensive
Automatic scholarships are modest — $2,000 for 3.5 GPA and 23 ACT
In-state tuition is a genuine bargain, especially for Hawaii residents
key_quotes: I would only be paying 10k a year to go to UH.
For an out-of-state applicant, I can get an automatic $2,000 scholarship for having a 3.5 GPA and a 23 ACT — is it possible to get more with a 3.7 and 32?
I qualify for the WUE discounted tuition rate but even so I am looking at quite a large chunk of money to attend.

key_points: Marine biology and oceanography programs are legit draws given location
Academic advisers exist but students don't know about them until too late
Perceived locally as a fallback school — 'if you can't get into mainland colleges'
key_quotes: I didn't know the college adviser existed. If I knew, I would have triple majored.
People here in Hawaii consider it a school that you go to if you can't get into any colleges on the mainland.
Not prestigious or known for its academics — it's really my guaranteed safety.

key_points: Communication with incoming students is poor — info buried on website
Housing sign-up deadlines are rigid and confusing for prospective students
You have to dig through the website constantly — nothing is proactively sent
key_quotes: They've gotten better about contacting us — well, not contacting us, but putting everything on the website. You definitely have to explore the website A LOT.
I read that if I cancel my housing arrangements I'd be charged upwards of $150 — I'm not sure if that's correct or if I'm reading the wrong section.
I applied to UH Manoa and got accepted, paid the application fee, and today I got an email that my application fee was refunded. I'm so confused.

key_points: Parking is a nightmare — 30 mins to find a spot, then a mile walk
Campus facilities are aging and largely unchanged for decades
Manoa Valley location means frequent rain; rest of island can be sunny
key_quotes: The worst thing about UH is parking. I don't want to pay for expensive UH parking so I parked far away sometimes 1 mile away — it took sometimes 30 minutes to find parking.
Looks like nothing has been kept up.
Facilities need major upgrades... simple things like that would attract more students.

key_points: Kinesiology grads struggling to find local work after graduation
Hawaii job market is limited — many graduates leave the islands
No strong alumni network or corporate recruiting presence mentioned
key_quotes: My best friend recently graduated from the University of Hawaii - Manoa, and is having a tough time finding work within the area of his study (Kinesiology).
The economic prospects for staying in HI are... not great.
It is not prestigious or known for its academics.

key_points: Exceptional value for Hawaii in-state students — hard to beat at ~$8K/year
Out-of-state costs make it hard to justify over better-ranked mainland schools
Cost of living in Honolulu is brutal and undercuts tuition savings fast
key_quotes: Would it be worth it to go out of state for 35-40k to Ohio State or University of Minnesota for finance, or to go to UH Manoa Honors college for 8k a year?
I received an offer to University of Hawaii Manoa with tuition completely covered.
Rent for my family luckily is 'cheap' considering we have a 2 bedroom place. But it's been getting increasingly more expensive to get food.

key_points: Chill, laid-back atmosphere — not a high-pressure academic environment
Strong sense of local Hawaiian culture that mainland students find enriching
Dream school for outsiders; seen as a fallback by locals — big perception gap
key_quotes: This is so funny because people want to go to UH Manoa but everyone here in Hawaii considers it a school you go to if you can't get into any colleges on the mainland.
I grew up on a farm in Kansas and had a blast in college in Hawaii. Great experience.
More mainland people need to attend UH — even for only a semester or two — to broaden their view of the world.

key_points: Graduation Rate: 56% six-year graduation rate raises serious red flags
Facilities: Dorms and campus infrastructure unchanged and unmaintained for decades
Perception Gap: Locals treat it as a last resort; outsiders romanticize the location
key_quotes: The 56% graduation rate is very sus.
Not much has changed since I went here in the 70s... it looks like nothing has been kept up.
People here in Hawaii consider it a school that you go to if you can't get into any colleges on the mainland.

key_points: Moped ownership is a game-changer for getting around and saving money
UH community college system gives same lower-div credits for a fraction of cost
East-West Center offers unique Pacific and Asia-focused research opportunities
key_quotes: I also owned a moped. It was a great thing to have — much easier to park and cheaper. I was able to go to Costco and even take a mini tour of the island.
University of Hawaii community college campuses give you the same lower div credits for a fraction of the cost — plus it all shows up in your STAR and Laulima.
I worked at SSRI and met people from all over the Pacific — a life changer for me.
